notes: Kastner's Aberdeen had called NT Server 4.0 'a giant step forward' in July 1996; this study questions enterprise enterprise-class readiness.
BackOffice suite (Exchange, SQL Server, SNA Server, Systems Management Server) central to Microsoft's enterprise pitch. Discontinued as bundle ca. 2002.
Scalability Day claimed 1.8M emails/day with 50,000 users/server; but Kastner noted this was not yet shipping and customers reported scaling beyond a few hundred users was problematic.
Demonstrated 1 billion transactions/day and terabyte satellite imagery; Kastner critiqued benchmarks as unrealistic and TPC-A based.
100M internet hits/day benchmark on single 2-processor Pentium Pro with NT; Kastner challenged realism of 24-hour even load distribution.
Kastner's assessment: 'real enterprise high-end legitimately remains the province of Unix, the AS/400, and even the mainframe.'
Named alongside Unix and mainframe as legitimately retaining the enterprise high-end; still sold as IBM i today.
SQL Server VLM support announced as future feature; Oracle and Informix already supported on Digital Unix/Alpha for over a year.
